title: Father's views about family; mother's broken collarbone and surgery
description: Father liberalized his views about family and gave her more freedom. Charging at stores. Father's ledger. Mother's broken collarbone and serious surgery.

title: Brother's death in the Philippines; parents favoritism; resentment of her brother's freedom
description: Brother's death in the Philippines. The children said mother favored boys, father girls. Her resentment of brother's freedom to go places; he was accident-prone.

title: Mother giving mittens to a poor family; sister getting very gold chaperoning
description: Mother managed to give a poor family mittens without them knowing. Sister got very cold chaperoning a sleighing party.

title: Poor families in Moscow; Use of first telephones and baths in town
description: Poor families. Use of first telephones and baths in town.

title: Stories about a man named Wild Davey; Dr. Watkins shooting
description: Wild Davey. Kids pretended he was after them and said he ate his dogs. Hotels fed him. Hearing news of Dr. Watkins shooting. Killing of Steffens.

title: Curiosity about red light district; father finding a diamond ring
description: Children's curiosity about red light district. Father found a diamond ring, probably dropped by someone who'd won it gambling.

title: Going to school in Moscow; mothers coming to visit unexpectedly
description: School in Moscow. Program on the last day of the year. Speaking pieces. Subjects. Mothers came into the classroom to visit, unexpectedly.

title: Reading as a child; assigned card number one in the library
description: Her reading as a youngster - Elsie Dinsmore books. Assigned card number one in the library.

title: Being a flower girl in a wedding; first cars and movie theaters in Moscow
description: Flower girl at wedding. First cars and movie theatre. Summer stock in Moscow.

title: Going to prep school and being interested in physical education; disliking home economics; wanting to be a doctor
description: Prep school. Interest in physical education. Father's limited view of what women could study. Dislike of home economics study. A woman teacher training to be a doctor inspired her to become a doctor, but father was strongly opposed.

title: Red Cross work in the flu epidemic
description: Red Cross work in the flu epidemic.
